== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:十市御縣主坐神社1.JPG|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Tōichi-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|51.6|N|135|48|28.1|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}}、{{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Tōichi
Miagata Nimasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine''' (とおちのみあがたにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The ''Great Yamato Province Tax Register'' of {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 3; {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}) records “Tōichi Mikata Kōbe,” showing it is an ancient shrine continuing from before the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}. It is a {{ill|lt=shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Associated deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} **According to shrine tradition, the deity is '''{{ill|lt=Ōme|Q97496325|WD=Q97496325|lt=Ōme|1=Toichi no Agatanushi Ōme}}''', ancestor of the Tōichi Agatanushi seen in the ''{{ill|lt=Kōrei Tennō-ki|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}''. Isasaka-hime, daughter of Tōichi Agatanushi Isasakahiko, was {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōan|Q312821|WD=Q312821|1=Emperor Kōan}}’s {{ill|lt=empress|Q7723211|WD=Q7723211|1=Empress consort}}; and {{ill|lt=Hosohime-no-Mikoto|Q11605816|WD=Q11605816|1=Kuwashi-hime}}, daughter of Ōme, was Empress of Emperor Kōrei. === History === The founding date is unknown, but it is an ancient shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}''. Since ancient times it was {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} {{ill|lt=Imperial property|Q11581043|WD=Q11581043|lt=land|1=imperial property}}, and worshiped the spirit of sacred vegetable gardens used to cultivate {{ill|lt=vegetables|Q952624|WD=Q952624|1=Olericulture}} offered to the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}. Together with Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōichi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu, it was one of the “{{ill|lt=Six Agata of Yamato|Q55521849|WD=Q55521849|lt=Six Mikata Shrines of Yamato Province|1=The Six Prefectures of Wa}},” deeply revered by the imperial court. On {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=January 27|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|1=Ten'an}} 3), its {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} rose to {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Upper|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and shrine households were allotted. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}}, under the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engi system|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and received official offerings at the Kinen, Tsukinami, and Niiname rites. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was also called “Jūsansho (Jūsasho) Daimyōjin,” but was identified with the Engishiki “Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine.” === Annual festivals === *{{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} *{{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Agatanushi|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487|1=Agatanushi}} === External links === * {{国立国会図書館のデジタル化資料|950814/108|Yamato shiryo, lower volume}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22808/ Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine - Shrine Materials Database] * {{Commonscat-inline|Tochinomiagatanimasu-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧村社]] [[Category:Toichi District]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

The ''Great Yamato Province Tax Register'' of {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 3; {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}) records “Tōichi Mikata Kōbe,” showing it is an ancient shrine continuing from before the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}. It is a {{ill|lt=shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Associated deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} **According to shrine tradition, the deity is '''{{ill|lt=Ōme|Q97496325|WD=Q97496325|lt=Ōme|1=Toichi no Agatanushi Ōme}}''', ancestor of the Tōichi Agatanushi seen in the ''{{ill|lt=Kōrei Tennō-ki|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}''. Isasaka-hime, daughter of Tōichi Agatanushi Isasakahiko, was {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōan|Q312821|WD=Q312821|1=Emperor Kōan}}’s {{ill|lt=empress|Q7723211|WD=Q7723211|1=Empress consort}}; and {{ill|lt=Hosohime-no-Mikoto|Q11605816|WD=Q11605816|1=Kuwashi-hime}}, daughter of Ōme, was Empress of Emperor Kōrei. === History === The founding date is unknown, but it is an ancient shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}''. Since ancient times it was {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} {{ill|lt=Imperial property|Q11581043|WD=Q11581043|lt=land|1=imperial property}}, and worshiped the spirit of sacred vegetable gardens used to cultivate {{ill|lt=vegetables|Q952624|WD=Q952624|1=Olericulture}} offered to the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}. Together with Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōichi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu, it was one of the “{{ill|lt=Six Agata of Yamato|Q55521849|WD=Q55521849|lt=Six Mikata Shrines of Yamato Province|1=The Six Prefectures of Wa}},” deeply revered by the imperial court. On {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=January 27|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|1=Ten'an}} 3), its {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} rose to {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Upper|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and shrine households were allotted. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}}, under the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engi system|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and received official offerings at the Kinen, Tsukinami, and Niiname rites. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was also called “Jūsansho (Jūsasho) Daimyōjin,” but was identified with the Engishiki “Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine.” === Annual festivals === *{{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} *{{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Agatanushi|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487|1=Agatanushi}} === External links === * {{国立国会図書館のデジタル化資料|950814/108|Yamato shiryo, lower volume}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22808/ Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine - Shrine Materials Database] * {{Commonscat-inline|Tochinomiagatanimasu-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Toichi District]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:十市御縣主坐神社1.JPG|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Tōichi-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|51.6|N|135|48|28.1|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}}、{{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Tōichi
Miagata Nimasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine''' (とおちのみあがたにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The ''Great Yamato Province Tax Register'' of {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 3; {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}) records “Tōichi Mikata Kōbe,” showing it is an ancient shrine continuing from before the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}. It is a {{ill|lt=shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Associated deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} **According to shrine tradition, the deity is '''{{ill|lt=Ōme|Q97496325|WD=Q97496325|lt=Ōme|1=Toichi no Agatanushi Ōme}}''', ancestor of the Tōichi Agatanushi seen in the ''{{ill|lt=Kōrei Tennō-ki|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}''. Isasaka-hime, daughter of Tōichi Agatanushi Isasakahiko, was {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōan|Q312821|WD=Q312821|1=Emperor Kōan}}’s {{ill|lt=empress|Q7723211|WD=Q7723211|1=Empress consort}}; and {{ill|lt=Hosohime-no-Mikoto|Q11605816|WD=Q11605816|1=Kuwashi-hime}}, daughter of Ōme, was Empress of Emperor Kōrei. === History === The founding date is unknown, but it is an ancient shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}''. Since ancient times it was {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} {{ill|lt=Imperial property|Q11581043|WD=Q11581043|lt=land|1=imperial property}}, and worshiped the spirit of sacred vegetable gardens used to cultivate {{ill|lt=vegetables|Q952624|WD=Q952624|1=Olericulture}} offered to the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}. Together with Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōichi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu, it was one of the “{{ill|lt=Six Agata of Yamato|Q55521849|WD=Q55521849|lt=Six Mikata Shrines of Yamato Province|1=The Six Prefectures of Wa}},” deeply revered by the imperial court. On {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=January 27|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|1=Ten'an}} 3), its {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} rose to {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Upper|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and shrine households were allotted. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}}, under the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engi system|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and received official offerings at the Kinen, Tsukinami, and Niiname rites. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was also called “Jūsansho (Jūsasho) Daimyōjin,” but was identified with the Engishiki “Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine.” === Annual festivals === *{{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} *{{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Agatanushi|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487|1=Agatanushi}} === External links === * {{国立国会図書館のデジタル化資料|950814/108|Yamato shiryo, lower volume}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22808/ Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine - Shrine Materials Database] * {{Commonscat-inline|Tochinomiagatanimasu-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Toichi District]] [[Category:橿原市の建築物]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

On {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=January 27|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|1=Ten'an}} 3), its {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} rose to {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Upper|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and shrine households were allotted. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}}, under the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engi system|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and received official offerings at the Kinen, Tsukinami, and Niiname rites. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was also called “Jūsansho (Jūsasho) Daimyōjin,” but was identified with the Engishiki “Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine.” === Annual festivals === *{{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} *{{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Agatanushi|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487|1=Agatanushi}} === External links === * {{国立国会図書館のデジタル化資料|950814/108|Yamato shiryo, lower volume}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22808/ Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine - Shrine Materials Database] * {{Commonscat-inline|Tochinomiagatanimasu-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Toichi District]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:十市御縣主坐神社1.JPG|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Tōichi-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|51.6|N|135|48|28.1|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}}、{{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Tōichi
Miagata Nimasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine''' (とおちのみあがたにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The ''Great Yamato Province Tax Register'' of {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 3; {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}) records “Tōichi Mikata Kōbe,” showing it is an ancient shrine continuing from before the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}. It is a {{ill|lt=shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Associated deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} **According to shrine tradition, the deity is '''{{ill|lt=Ōme|Q97496325|WD=Q97496325|lt=Ōme|1=Toichi no Agatanushi Ōme}}''', ancestor of the Tōichi Agatanushi seen in the ''{{ill|lt=Kōrei Tennō-ki|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}''. Isasaka-hime, daughter of Tōichi Agatanushi Isasakahiko, was {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōan|Q312821|WD=Q312821|1=Emperor Kōan}}’s {{ill|lt=empress|Q7723211|WD=Q7723211|1=Empress consort}}; and {{ill|lt=Hosohime-no-Mikoto|Q11605816|WD=Q11605816|1=Kuwashi-hime}}, daughter of Ōme, was Empress of Emperor Kōrei. === History === The founding date is unknown, but it is an ancient shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}''. Since ancient times it was {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} {{ill|lt=Imperial property|Q11581043|WD=Q11581043|lt=land|1=imperial property}}, and worshiped the spirit of sacred vegetable gardens used to cultivate {{ill|lt=vegetables|Q952624|WD=Q952624|1=Olericulture}} offered to the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}. Together with Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōichi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu, it was one of the “{{ill|lt=Six Agata of Yamato|Q55521849|WD=Q55521849|lt=Six Mikata Shrines of Yamato Province|1=The Six Prefectures of Wa}},” deeply revered by the imperial court. On {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=January 27|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|1=Ten'an}} 3), its {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} rose to {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Upper|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and shrine households were allotted. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}}, under the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engi system|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and received official offerings at the Kinen, Tsukinami, and Niiname rites. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was also called “Jūsansho (Jūsasho) Daimyōjin,” but was identified with the Engishiki “Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine.” === Annual festivals === *{{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} *{{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Agatanushi|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487|1=Agatanushi}} === External links === * {{国立国会図書館のデジタル化資料|950814/108|Yamato shiryo, lower volume}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22808/ Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine - Shrine Materials Database] * {{Commonscat-inline|Tochinomiagatanimasu-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Toichi District]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]

== Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:十市御縣主坐神社1.JPG|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} Tōichi-chō 1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|51.6|N|135|48|28.1|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（great）
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}}、{{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Tōichi
Miagata Nimasu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine''' (とおちのみあがたにますじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara|Q496319|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The ''Great Yamato Province Tax Register'' of {{ill|lt=730|Q23563|WD=Q23563|1=730}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 3; {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}) records “Tōichi Mikata Kōbe,” showing it is an ancient shrine continuing from before the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|WD=Q189178|1=Nara period}}. It is a {{ill|lt=shikinaisha great shrine|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *Main deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} *Associated deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} **According to shrine tradition, the deity is '''{{ill|lt=Ōme|Q97496325|WD=Q97496325|lt=Ōme|1=Toichi no Agatanushi Ōme}}''', ancestor of the Tōichi Agatanushi seen in the ''{{ill|lt=Kōrei Tennō-ki|Q313041|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}''. Isasaka-hime, daughter of Tōichi Agatanushi Isasakahiko, was {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōan|Q312821|WD=Q312821|1=Emperor Kōan}}’s {{ill|lt=empress|Q7723211|WD=Q7723211|1=Empress consort}}; and {{ill|lt=Hosohime-no-Mikoto|Q11605816|WD=Q11605816|1=Kuwashi-hime}}, daughter of Ōme, was Empress of Emperor Kōrei. === History === The founding date is unknown, but it is an ancient shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}''. Since ancient times it was {{ill|lt=Imperial Household|Q909452|WD=Q909452|1=Imperial House of Japan}} {{ill|lt=Imperial property|Q11581043|WD=Q11581043|lt=land|1=imperial property}}, and worshiped the spirit of sacred vegetable gardens used to cultivate {{ill|lt=vegetables|Q952624|WD=Q952624|1=Olericulture}} offered to the {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}. Together with Takachi, Katsuraki, Tōichi, Shiki, Yamabe, and Sofu, it was one of the “{{ill|lt=Six Agata of Yamato|Q55521849|WD=Q55521849|lt=Six Mikata Shrines of Yamato Province|1=The Six Prefectures of Wa}},” deeply revered by the imperial court. On {{ill|lt=January 27 (lunar)|Q839032|WD=Q839032|lt=January 27|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)}}, {{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}} ({{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|1=Ten'an}} 3), its {{ill|lt=court rank for shrines|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} rose to {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Upper|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} and shrine households were allotted. In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|WD=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}}, under the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engi system|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it was listed as a {{ill|lt=great shrine|Q11438310|WD=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} and received official offerings at the Kinen, Tsukinami, and Niiname rites. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was also called “Jūsansho (Jūsasho) Daimyōjin,” but was identified with the Engishiki “Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine.” === Annual festivals === *{{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} *{{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Agatanushi|Q10746487|WD=Q10746487|1=Agatanushi}} === External links === * {{国立国会図書館のデジタル化資料|950814/108|Yamato shiryo, lower volume}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22808/ Tōichi Mikata-ni-masu Shrine - Shrine Materials Database] * {{Commonscat-inline|Tochinomiagatanimasu-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tōchi no Miagata Nimasu Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Toichi District]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]]
